column1: Every implementation task pulled from across the workbook into one ordered checklist. This is the build plan for the SHOAPPNATIVE repo and the Firestore backend. Priority: P0 = required for a safe launch (nothing generates for real users without these); P1 = core program engine; P2 = enhancement, can follow launch. 'Spec sheet' is where the rules live; build against that sheet, do not reinvent the logic here. Tick 'status' as you go.

column4: Task
column7: Acceptance check

column4: Build the Firestore collections: users, programs, workout_instances, set_logs, progression_state, swap_history, flags, plus users.planned_absences[].
column7: All collections exist with the exact field names from the spec. A read/write round-trips for each.

column4: Import the 110 exercises with every column (incl. Measurement Type, Load Unit, Stress Regions, equipment tags, Min RIR, Failure Allowed, Spotter).
column7: 110 docs seeded. Spot-check 5 exercises match the sheet exactly.

column4: Import the 528-row Substitutions collection (ID-based).
column7: Every substitute_id resolves to a real exercise. No dangling IDs.

column4: Build the canonical mapping lookup (onboarding answer -> backend value) as ONE shared module used by both the wizard and the generator.
column7: Two users with identical answers produce identical stored values. No AI inference in this path.

column4: Build the 11-step wizard, incl. date_of_birth (not age), trains_alone, weekday picker, equipment tags, injury chips, outside activity, and the final safety check.
column7: Every 'ask' field is collected. Nothing defaults age. Safety check cannot be skipped.

column4: Implement screening routing: 7 questions + joint follow-ups -> CLEAR / MODIFY / REQUIRE_CLEARANCE / DO_NOT_GENERATE. Block generation on the last two.
column7: A cardiac/pregnancy/at-rest flag produces NO program and the clearance message. A minor joint flag routes to MODIFY.

column4: Implement Age Routing: adult vs 16-17 youth path (guardian consent) vs under-16 block, from date_of_birth.
column7: A 17-year-old cannot enter the adult flow. Under-16 is blocked.

column4: Implement the Safety Rules engine: apply HARD_SAFETY rules after every prescription and every runtime change, in priority order. HARD rules cannot be overridden by user input.
column7: No output ever breaches a floor (reps<4 on Load, >88% 1RM, past Min RIR, no-spotter solo lift, advanced lift to non-advanced user).

column4: Implement stop-symptom escalation (S06): end session, log event, halt generation until resolved/cleared. Wire into the coach so it never 'swaps' past a symptom.
column6: Safety Rules S06, Exercise Swaps SW03
column7: A reported chest pain/dizziness ends the session and blocks regeneration.

column4: Wire the Coach AI Operating Rules as the system-prompt contract. extraInstructions points here; hard nevers and consult order enforced.
column7: The coach refuses out-of-scope asks, follows the consult order, and never breaches a hard never in testing.

column4: Accredited exercise professional signs off Screening Outcomes, Safety Rules, Age Routing, Injury Modifications, Coach AI Operating Rules before the coach is enabled.
column6: Screening Outcomes, Safety Rules, Age Routing, Injury Modifications, Coach AI Operating Rules
column7: Written sign-off on file. Coach stays disabled until this is done.

column4: Implement the 14-step Generator Flow as the build pipeline.
column7: A full program generates end-to-end for a CLEAR user across all 4 goals.

column4: Implement Split Selector (days x experience x goal -> split_id, first match wins).
column7: Every days(1-6) x experience x goal combo resolves to a valid split.

column4: Implement Session Templates slot-filling with token filters (Squat|Lunge, Compound>Isolation, FOCAL_1/2).
column7: Each day type fills required slots from valid candidates; optional slots drop on short sessions.

column4: Implement Weekly Volume budgeting + the 7 reconciliation rules (fill up, add slot, trim overshoot, focal, commitment cuts, floor).
column7: An Intermediate Hypertrophy UL4 user reaches 10-16 chest sets/week; PPL6 back is trimmed under 16.

column4: Implement Prescription Logic lookup (Goal|Class -> sets/reps/rir/rest/pct1rm), clamped by Min RIR + Failure Allowed + Safety Rules.
column7: Every prescribed exercise reads its grid row and respects its Min RIR.

column4: Implement Schedule Rules: rest floor, 48h spacing, placement order, commitment adjacency, missed-session SCH07/08, exam SCH10.
column7: A basketball-Monday user gets legs kept away from Monday and one guaranteed rest day.

column4: Implement the Progression Engine: adjust reps/sets/load from set_logs within grid ranges + equipment increments; pause on fatigue, reverse on missed reps.
column7: Hitting reps_max at target RIR for 2 sessions triggers a rounded load increase; a stall triggers a deload.

column4: Implement Exercise Swaps: walk Substitutions to first compatible option; persist dislike/pain exclusions; re-prescribe and re-clamp.
column7: A dislike swap never re-serves the exercise; a pain swap avoids the stress region.

column4: Implement Goal Change: re-run split/volume/prescription, preserve loads + history, one easy transition week, version the program.
column7: Changing goal keeps logged loads, creates a new program version, does not spike volume >20%.

column4: Implement Custom Splits + Custom Split Resolution (fuzzy request -> day_structure -> validate coverage/spacing/shared volume).
column6: Custom Split Rules, Custom Split Resolution
column7: 'Legs once a week' on 4 days resolves deterministically to Push|Pull|Upper|Lower and passes coverage.

column4: Implement Injury Modifications: apply the flagged region row (exclude/downgrade/prefer/rir_bump) to every session; review_after to lift.
column7: A cleared lower-back flag removes free-weight spinal loading and adds +1 RIR to back-loading work.

column4: Implement Exam Survival Protocol: date-range + mode picker, range-aware generation, auto-restore + re-entry ramp, penalty suppression.
column6: Exam Survival Protocol, Exam Protocol Build Spec
column7: Declaring an exam range applies the chosen mode, protects loads, and restores automatically.

column4: Populate exercise media: video_url, thumbnail, captions for all 110 exercises.
column7: Every exercise screen shows a form video. No blank media in the live library.

column4: Formalise the equipment taxonomy (equipment_id, equivalent_ids, required_all/required_any) to replace slash/comma tag strings.
column7: Equipment filtering uses structured any/all groups, not string parsing.

column4: Build conversational entry points for goal change, swaps, exam ranges and preferences (natural-language -> the right rule).
column7: A user can trigger each runtime feature by chat, and it routes through the consult order.

column4: Pre-range and restore prompts; deload prompt at 6 weeks; missed-streak nudge.
column6: Exam Protocol Build Spec, Safety Rules F01
column7: User receives the right nudge at the right time; none are punitive.

column4: Build the profile-sweep test harness: generate programs across hundreds of user profiles and assert no safety breach and no empty required slot.
column7: Automated suite runs on every backend change; zero safety-rule violations across the sweep.

column2: Required for a safe launch. Nothing generates for real users until every P0 item, including professional sign-off, is done.
Core program engine. The app builds and adapts real programs once these are done.
Enhancements. Improve quality, content and safety coverage; can follow the initial launch.
